events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose fault: tile on single-layer subfloor deflection. In-service. Residential. Tile on single-layer subfloor deflection likely means excessive deflection causing cracked tile/grout, maybe due to subfloor inadequate stiffness, joist span, underlayment missing. Need apply mfr instructions and TCNA. TCNA requires subfloor deflection limits L/360 for ceramic tile, L/720 for natural stone. Also requires proper underlayment/second layer or suitable subfloor. Single-layer subfloor may be insufficient; tile needs minimum subfloor thickness and a tile backer or second layer. Deflection indicates non-compliance. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? Not dangerous but non-compliant and can lead to failure. The prompt says if work dangerous or non-compliant, decision must fail. So fail. Risk medium/high? Tile cracking, trip hazard, water intrusion. In-service degraded/failed. Component conditions: degraded, failed maybe installed-defective, non-compliant. Need include applicable categories from list: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. I'd choose non-compliant, degraded, failed. percent_complete 100 (in-service installed). value maybe deflection ratio? Not asked numeric reading. Could leave 0. confidence high 0.9. references: TCNA Handbook, ANSI A108/A118/A136, manufacturer instructions. Findings: tile installed over single-layer subfloor with excessive deflection; subfloor does not meet L/360 deflection limit; no second layer/underlayment per TCNA/mfr. Actions: evaluate joist span and subfloor thickness; add sister joists/blocking or install second layer/cement backer; replace cracked/loose tile; re-grout/seal. Rationale brief. Need ensure JSON valid.

events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "degraded", "failed"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Tile flooring installed over a single-layer subfloor exhibits excessive deflection.",
    "Subfloor assembly does not meet the stiffness/deflection limits required by TCNA and tile manufacturer instructions.",
    "Deflection has caused or will cause cracked grout, cracked/bond-failed tile, and trip hazards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify joist span, spacing, and subfloor thickness against TCNA and manufacturer requirements.",
    "Reduce deflection by sistering joists, adding blocking, or otherwise reinforcing the framing to meet L/360 (or L/720 for stone).",
    "Install a proper second layer or tile backer/underlayment per manufacturer and TCNA methods before re-tiling.",
    "Remove and replace cracked, loose, or hollow-sounding tile and re-grout/seal the installation."
  ],
  "rationale": "Tile assemblies require a stiff, minimally deflecting subfloor. A single-layer subfloor with visible/audible deflection violates TCNA deflection limits and manufacturer installation instructions, leading to grout/tile failure and safety hazards.",
  "references": [
    "TCNA Handbook for Ceramic, Glass, and Stone Tile Installation - subfloor deflection requirements",
    "ANSI A108/A118/A136 installation standards",
    "Tile and setting-material manufacturer installation instructions"
  ]
}
